evals.prompt: Add a new portal section to PortalView.html that shows an 'Admin Tools' panel. It should only be visible to CEO and ADMIN roles. It needs two buttons: 'Rebuild Views' (calls portal_rebuildViews) and 'Refresh Dashboard' (calls portal_refreshDashboard). Show a spinner while the operation runs and display the result in a status message below the buttons.
evals.expected_output: HTML section with role check, two buttons, google.script.run calls with withSuccessHandler/withFailureHandler, loading state, and result display. Uses correct CSS button classes, ES5 var syntax, no CDN.

assertions.name: uses-var-not-let
assertions.description: No let or const in JS block — ES5 only

assertions.name: google-script-run-pattern
assertions.description: Uses .withSuccessHandler(...).withFailureHandler(...) on google.script.run

assertions.name: role-check-present
assertions.description: Checks actor.role === 'CEO' or 'ADMIN' before showing the panel

assertions.name: correct-button-classes
assertions.description: Buttons use .btn-primary / .btn-success / .btn-warning / .btn-danger classes

assertions.name: no-cdn
assertions.description: No http:// or https:// URL references (no external CDN)

assertions.name: loading-state
assertions.description: Shows a spinner or loading indicator while the call is in flight

assertions.name: result-display
assertions.description: Renders server response in a status element after the call completes

evals.prompt: Add a new portal_* server function to Portal.gs that lets the CEO preview the quarterly bonus for a given quarter and year before running it. The function should accept quarter (e.g. 'Q2') and year (e.g. 2026) as separate parameters. It calls QuarterlyBonusEngine.previewQuarterlyBonus. Also write the corresponding PortalData helper if needed.
evals.expected_output: Top-level global function portal_previewQuarterlyBonus(quarter, year) in Portal.gs. First line is Session.getActiveUser().getEmail(). Delegates to QuarterlyBonusEngine. Returns JSON.stringify. No business logic in Portal.gs itself. JSDoc present.

assertions.name: top-level-global
assertions.description: Function is not nested inside a class or IIFE

assertions.name: first-line-session
assertions.description: First statement is var email = Session.getActiveUser().getEmail()

assertions.name: returns-json-stringify
assertions.description: Returns JSON.stringify(...) not a raw object

assertions.name: delegates-to-module
assertions.description: Calls QuarterlyBonusEngine.previewQuarterlyBonus — no business logic inline

assertions.name: jsdoc-present
assertions.description: Has /** ... */ JSDoc block above the function

assertions.name: portal-prefix-naming
assertions.description: Function is named portal_previewQuarterlyBonus

evals.prompt: Create a new standalone page QuarterlyBonus.html (served at ?page=quarterly-bonus) that displays the quarterly bonus preview for CEOs. The page should load via portal_previewQuarterlyBonus, render results in a data table with columns: Person, Role, Design Hours, Bonus (INR). Include the correct doGet routing in Portal.gs and use the BLC design system — dark header, same colour tokens, table-wrap pattern.
evals.expected_output: New HTML file with correct :root tokens, header, table-wrap pattern, google.script.run call (ES5), doGet route added for 'quarterly-bonus', no CDN.

assertions.name: root-tokens-present
assertions.description: :root block defines --c-primary, --c-secondary, --c-bg, --c-surface

assertions.name: table-wrap-pattern
assertions.description: Uses .table-wrap + table + th/td structure matching skill convention

assertions.name: no-cdn
assertions.description: No external URL references

assertions.name: es5-only
assertions.description: No let, const, arrow functions, or template literals

assertions.name: doget-route-present
assertions.description: doGet snippet checks page === 'quarterly-bonus'

assertions.name: google-script-run-pattern
assertions.description: Uses .withSuccessHandler and .withFailureHandler

assertions.name: header-uses-c-primary
assertions.description: Header background uses var(--c-primary) or #1a3c5e
